mission.title: spire: Smarter Authorized Entry Caching
mission.description: Smarter Authorized Entry Caching. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify spire feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your spire deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app=spire
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Smarter Authorized Entry Caching

steps.title: Check current Deployment configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire resources:
```bash
kubectl get deployment -A
kubectl describe deployment <name> -n <namespace>
```
In 0.12.0 we introduced a full in-memory authorized entry cache. This greatly reduced the server load in large deployments by doing a single full table scan of the entries and attested nodes just once

steps.title: Apply the fix for Smarter Authorized Entry Caching
steps.description:
**Pull Request check list**

**Affected functionality**
SPIRE entry cache

**Description of change**
Uses an event in sql to build the cache. Rather than fetch the whole table every 5 seconds, get only the events that occurred since the last fetch.

Creates a new table in the sql database and a bunch of triggers. For example when a new entry is created the trigger will create an entry in the entry events table. SPIRE will read this table and update the in memory cache.

Code to prune the event t

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Confirm Smarter Authorized Entry Caching is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire -n cert-manager --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: **Pull Request check list**

**Affected functionality**
SPIRE entry cache

**Description of change**
Uses an event in sql to build the cache. Rather than fetch the whole table every 5 seconds, get only the events that occurred since the last fetch.

Creates a new table in the sql database and a bunch of triggers. For example when a new entry is created the trigger will create an entry in the entry. This fixes the issue because it addresses the underlying problem that was causing the failure.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
